skill.name: outlier-detection-handler
skill.description: Identify and manage statistical outliers in datasets using Z-score, IQR, and Grubbs test methods for data quality control and regulatory compliance.
skill.summary: Statistical outlier detection and handling skill for data analysis workflows with structured execution and clear output boundaries.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 43 static analysis findings were evaluated and dismissed as false positives. The 'system reconnaissance' and 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings matched non-executable content in JSON metadata and markdown documentation, not actual code. The 'path traversal' findings matched security checklist items that explicitly prohibit directory traversal. The 'external commands' findings matched markdown code blocks showing example bash commands for running the python script, which is documented and expected skill behavior. The skill is a legitimate data analysis tool using numpy and scipy for statistical outlier detection. No malicious patterns, data exfiltration, credential leaks, or prompt injection attempts were detected.

content.user_title: Detect and manage statistical outliers in datasets
content.value_statement: Data analysts often struggle to identify and handle outliers consistently across projects. This skill provides a structured workflow with three detection methods and clear handling recommendations for reproducible data quality control.
actual_capabilities: Detect outliers using Z-score, IQR, or Grubbs test methods
Generate handling recommendations based on outlier percentage
Process datasets from text files with one numeric value per line
Produce structured output with method details and recommendations
Support data quality control and pre-analysis screening workflows
limitations: Only supports one-dimensional numeric data, not multivariate outlier detection
Does not handle missing values or non-numeric data automatically
Grubbs test implementation detects a single outlier per pass only
No built-in visualization or plotting capabilities

use_cases.title: Clinical trial data quality control
use_cases.description: A clinical research associate needs to screen biomarker measurements from 200 patients for data entry errors before statistical analysis.

use_cases.title: Laboratory instrument data validation
use_cases.description: A lab technician must identify anomalous readings from automated instruments to prevent contaminated data from entering the analysis pipeline.

use_cases.title: Regulatory submission data integrity
use_cases.description: A data manager preparing FDA submission documents needs documented outlier detection with explicit assumptions for regulatory compliance.

prompt_templates.title: Basic outlier detection with default settings
prompt_templates.prompt: Run outlier detection on the data file at path/to/data.txt using the default IQR method. Flag any outliers found and provide a handling recommendation.

prompt_templates.title: Custom method with Z-score threshold
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze dataset.csv for outliers using the Z-score method with a threshold of 2.5. Remove detected outliers and output the cleaned dataset.

prompt_templates.title: Comparative method analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Run all three outlier detection methods Z-score, IQR, and Grubbs test on patient_biomarkers.txt and compare the results. Provide a recommendation on which method is most appropriate for this dataset.

prompt_templates.title: Regulatory compliance workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Process lab_results.csv for outliers using the IQR method with winsorization. Document all assumptions, thresholds, and handling decisions in a format suitable for regulatory audit review.

output_examples.input: Biomarker measurements from 200 patients processed through IQR outlier detection
output_examples.output: 5 outliers identified (2.5% of data). IQR bounds: [12.34, 87.65]. Recommendation: Investigate outliers - may be legitimate extreme values.

output_examples.input: Sensor readings with a known extreme value using Z-score method at threshold 3
output_examples.output: 1 outlier detected (value 99.8 at index 7, Z-score 4.2). Recommendation: Remove outlier - likely data entry error. Mean without outlier: 14.2 versus mean with outlier: 18.5.

best_practices: Review the data distribution before choosing a detection method. IQR works well for skewed distributions while Z-score assumes normality.
Document all parameters and assumptions used during outlier detection to ensure reproducibility in regulated environments.
Start with the flag action to review outliers before applying remove or winsorize actions to avoid unintentional data loss.
anti_patterns: Do not remove outliers automatically without understanding the domain context. Outliers may represent real phenomena or equipment issues.
Do not apply outlier detection to datasets with fewer than 10 data points. Statistical methods lose reliability with small sample sizes.
Do not use the same threshold across different datasets without verifying that the underlying distributions are comparable.

faq.question: What detection methods are available in this skill?
faq.answer: Three methods are supported: Z-score with configurable threshold, IQR with the standard 1.5x rule, and a simplified Grubbs test for single outlier detection.

faq.question: What file formats are supported for input data?
faq.answer: The script reads text files with one numeric value per line. CSV and Excel support is planned but not yet implemented.

faq.question: How does the skill handle missing or invalid data?
faq.answer: The implementation skips empty lines but does not handle non-numeric values. Validate and clean your data before running outlier detection.

faq.question: Can this skill be used for multivariate outlier detection?
faq.answer: No. This skill only handles one-dimensional numeric data. For multivariate outlier detection consider methods like Mahalanobis distance.

faq.question: What is the recommended workflow for regulatory compliance?
faq.answer: Document all parameters, assumptions, and detection thresholds. Use the flag action first, review results manually, then apply handling actions.

faq.question: How do I choose between the three detection methods?
faq.answer: Use Z-score for normally distributed data, IQR for skewed distributions, and Grubbs test for a formal statistical test on a single extreme value.
